conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately, overall?
I don't know... just tired, I suppose. I have a lot on my plate, but physically I feel drained. It's hard to explain.
Can you tell me more about what feeling drained is like for you?
It's like I'm running out of steam. I try to do things, but my body just feels heavy. Even small things, like walking up the stairs, feel like climbing a mountain.
Is it harder to focus or concentrate than it used to be?
Yes, definitely. I'll be reading something and suddenly I'll just zone out. I look up and I've read the same sentence three times without noticing. It's like my brain isn't really working anymore.
How long does that feeling of zoning out usually last when it happens?
It depends. Sometimes it snaps back into place after a minute, and sometimes it lasts for hours. I never know when it's going to happen, so I just try to sit there quietly until it passes.
How have you been sleeping lately?
I haven't been sleeping well at all. I lie there for hours, just listening to the clock. I don't know if it's stress or just getting older, but I feel like I'm losing my ability to relax.
When you say you feel like you're losing your ability to relax, what does that feel like for you?
It's like I'm always waiting for something to go wrong. Even when everything seems fine, I'm waiting for the other shoe to drop. It's hard to explain, but I just feel on edge all the time.
Been doing anything you enjoy lately?
Not really. I used to love gardening, but I haven't had the energy for it in months. I just feel like I'm just existing right now, not really living.
What's it like to feel like you're just existing?
It's just... quiet, I suppose. Like I'm just going through the motions. I used to be the person who always had a plan, but now I don't even know what I want to do tomorrow.
When you say this will end soon, are you talking about ending your life?
I... I don't know. I suppose I'm hoping that things will get better. I don't want to think about it ending, but sometimes it feels like the easiest option.
Have you thought about specific steps or a specific way you would do it?
No, I haven't. I don't want to think about it that way. I just... I don't know. It feels like the thought is just there, but I don't know if I can actually do it.
When did this sense of emptiness start feeling this strong?
I suppose it started a few months ago. It's hard to pinpoint exactly when, but I just started feeling like I was just going through the motions.
What parts of life feel most difficult or empty lately?
Everything. I don't know how to put my finger on it, but just... everything feels empty. I don't know if it's just me or if this is what middle age is supposed to be like.
How do you see the next few weeks unfolding for you?
I don't know. Just more of the same, I suppose. I don't see anything changing soon.
How much of this feels like giving up versus feeling exhausted?
I don't know if it's one or the other. It's just... I don't have the energy to fight it anymore.
Do you feel like people would be better off without you, or is it more that you feel stuck?
I don't know if people would be better off, but I just feel stuck. I don't know how to get out of this hole, and I don't know if anyone can help me.
When you think about the next few weeks, what do you expect day to day?
Just more of the same, I suppose. I don't see anything changing.
Does "more of the same" feel pretty heavy to carry?
Yes. It feels like I'm just carrying a weight, but I don't know how to put it down.
Do you find it harder to make decisions than before, even small ones?
Yes. I look at a menu and I can't decide what to eat. It feels like I'm losing my ability to think.
How long has it been since you could easily choose something from a menu?
I don't know... years, I suppose. It used to be easy, but now I just freeze up.
Do you feel like you have enough energy for your usual day?
No, not really. I wake up and I'm just... done. I don't know how to get the energy back.
How long has it felt this way when you first wake up?
A few months now. It used to be just a morning thing, but now it feels like it's here to stay.
Are you still into the things you used to care about?
No. I look at them, and I just... I don't know. I used to love reading, but now I just can't focus.
How long has it been hard to focus on reading?
A few months, I suppose. I used to devour books in a weekend, but now I can't even get through a chapter.
Is there anything weighing on your mind that you wish you could change?
I wish I could go back to when everything was easy. I don't know if that's possible, though.
What feels most difficult now compared to when things were easy?
Just... everything. I don't know how to make things easy again.
How do you feel about how things have gone for you recently?
I don't know... I just feel like I've failed, I suppose.
What does that feeling of failure come from for you?
I don't know if I'm supposed to say this, but... I used to be the one who fixed things. Now I'm just the one who needs fixing.
How's your appetite been?
I don't have much of an appetite. I eat when I have to, but I don't really enjoy it anymore.
Has this change in how food tastes or feels been going on for a while?
A few months, I suppose. It's hard to say.
Have you found yourself getting emotional more easily lately?
Yes. I get upset over small things and I don't know why.
